title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reconciled sprite and group lifecycle and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
Use separate update, draw-layer, collision, projectile, hazard, and UI groups only when ownership and membership are explicit.
Keep group update inputs consistent or route context through an explicit system boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Sprite Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 4374 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.

Inspect the Sprite Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Sprite Contract.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Sprite Contract, keep Sprite Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Layered Groups
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-layered-groups-1dj7r7" that uses case 3668 to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Layered Groups.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Layered Groups into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Layered Groups, keep Layered Groups connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-layered-groups-1dj7r7
# Competency: asteroids-layered-groups.
# Case 3668: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_layered_groups_1dj7r7(created: int = 20, active: int = 13, killed: int = 7, group_memberships_released: bool = True, update_before_draw: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1dj7r7 = "1dj7r7-7r7jd1-3668"
    assert evidence_variant_1dj7r7
    evidence_axes_1dj7r7 = (
        "player1dj7r7",
        "seed3668",
        "input7r7jd1",
        "frame1dj7r73668",
        "state36681dj7r7",
        "playtest7r7jd13668",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1dj7r7)) == 6
    if min(created, active, killed) < 0:
        return False, active, "group-domain"
    if active + killed != created:
        return False, active, "lifecycle-reconciliation"
    if not group_memberships_released:
        return False, killed, "group-leak"
    if not update_before_draw:
        return False, active, "stale-render"
    assert active + killed == created
    return True, active, "owned-sprites"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.
